reasoning: In tls_construct_server_hello (ssl/statem/statem_srvr.c:2577-2820), the code explicitly handles the legacy_version field for TLS 1.3. Lines 2583-2587 show: 'int usetls13 = SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s) || s->hello_retry_request == SSL_HRR_PENDING; version = usetls13 ? TLS1_2_VERSION : s->version; if (!WPACKET_put_bytes_u16(pkt, version)'. When TLS 1.3 is being negotiated (SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s) is true), usetls13 is true, and version is set to TLS1_2_VERSION which is 0x0303 in OpenSSL. This value is then written as the first u16 field (legacy_version) of the ServerHello message. This covers both the normal TLS 1.3 ServerHello path and the HelloRetryRequest path (SSL_HRR_PENDING), both of which correctly set legacy_version to 0x0303.

evidence.expected_behavior: The server MUST set the legacy_version field in ServerHello to 0x0303 (TLS 1.2) when negotiating TLS 1.3.
